purpose: Models the degradation of cylindrical roller bearings that handle heavy radial loads in the spindle assembly. Tracks fatigue life, grease condition, and cage wear as primary degradation mechanisms, and raises fault events for raceway flaking, scoring, rib damage, excessive preload, and cage noise.

inputs.name: lubrication_film
inputs.description: Lubricant volume delivered to each bearing; governs grease condition and film-thickness-dependent wear mechanisms

states.name: bearing_fatigue_life
states.description: Remaining fatigue life of the cylindrical roller bearing, degrading from 1.0 (new) toward 0.0 (end-of-life) driven by accumulated radial load cycles and rotational speed. Represents progressive sub-surface fatigue leading to raceway flaking.

states.name: grease_condition_degradation
states.description: Health of the grease lubricant inside the bearing, degrading from 1.0 (fresh grease) toward 0.0 (fully degraded) due to thermal stress, oxidation, and contamination from brass cage wear particles. Low values indicate loss of film-forming capacity.

states.name: cage_material_wear
states.description: Structural integrity of the brass roller cage, degrading from 1.0 (intact) toward 0.0 (severely worn) under severe operating conditions including inadequate lubrication and high contact stresses. Cage wear generates metallic particles that accelerate grease degradation.

states.name: lubrication_film_tracked
states.description: First-order lag tracking state for the lubrication_film input. Used as a threshold reference in event logic to avoid referencing raw inputs directly in event conditions.

events.name: flaking_one_side_raceway
events.description: Sub-surface fatigue has progressed to the point where material flaking occurs on one side of the radial bearing raceway. Triggered when bearing_fatigue_life degrades below the critical fatigue threshold, indicating end-of-life fatigue damage.

events.name: scoring_raceway_rolling_surface
events.description: Scoring or smearing between the raceway and rolling element surfaces due to lubricant film breakdown. Triggered when grease condition is severely degraded and lubrication film delivery falls below the minimum adequate film threshold.

events.name: rib_scoring
events.description: Scoring or smearing between the end faces of rollers and the guide rib, caused by combined grease degradation and cage wear increasing roller skew and axial contact forces.

events.name: excessive_preload
events.description: Excessive bearing preload condition leading toward seizure, arising when fatigue-induced radial clearance loss and cage wear combine to eliminate internal clearance. Triggered when both bearing_fatigue_life and cage_material_wear exceed their respective critical degradation thresholds.

events.name: cage_noise
events.description: Audible cage noise in grease-lubricated bearings caused by cage instability from wear and insufficient grease damping. Triggered when cage_material_wear and grease_condition_degradation both exceed their maintenance-level thresholds.

parameters.name: bearing_wear_rate
parameters.description: Rate constant governing bearing fatigue life degradation per unit driver activity per time step. Sourced from simulator ground truth.

parameters.name: grease_degradation_rate
parameters.description: Rate constant governing grease condition degradation per unit thermal stress and oxidation driver per time step. Sourced from simulator ground truth.

parameters.name: contamination_buildup_rate
parameters.description: Rate constant governing cage material wear degradation driven by metallic wear particle accumulation per time step. Sourced from simulator ground truth (contamination_buildup_rate).

parameters.name: critical_bearing_wear
parameters.description: Fractional degradation of bearing_fatigue_life (measured as 1 - remaining life) above which raceway flaking and excessive preload events are triggered. Sourced from simulator ground truth.

parameters.name: critical_grease_degradation
parameters.description: Fractional degradation of grease_condition_degradation (measured as 1 - health) above which scoring and rib scoring events are triggered.

parameters.name: critical_cage_wear
parameters.description: Fractional degradation of cage_material_wear (measured as 1 - health) above which cage noise and excessive preload events are triggered.

parameters.name: min_adequate_lubrication_film
parameters.description: Minimum lubrication film delivery (cc/brg) below which scoring events become active. Used as threshold for lubrication_film_tracked state.

parameters.name: lubrication_film_lag_coefficient
parameters.description: First-order lag coefficient for lubrication_film_tracked state tracking the lubrication_film input. Capped at 0.5 for numerical stability.

parameters.name: cage_noise_grease_threshold
parameters.description: Fractional degradation of grease_condition_degradation above which cage noise event becomes active in combination with cage wear.

parameters.name: bearing_fatigue_life_min
parameters.description: Minimum allowable value for bearing_fatigue_life state (fully degraded).

parameters.name: bearing_fatigue_life_max
parameters.description: Maximum allowable value for bearing_fatigue_life state (new condition).

parameters.name: grease_condition_min
parameters.description: Minimum allowable value for grease_condition_degradation state (fully degraded grease).

parameters.name: grease_condition_max
parameters.description: Maximum allowable value for grease_condition_degradation state (fresh grease).

parameters.name: cage_material_wear_min
parameters.description: Minimum allowable value for cage_material_wear state (fully worn cage).

parameters.name: cage_material_wear_max
parameters.description: Maximum allowable value for cage_material_wear state (intact cage).

state_transition_logic: bearing_fatigue_life: Decrease by bearing_wear_rate * load_cycles_and_speed_driver * dt, where load_cycles_and_speed_driver is the normalized load-cycle-and-speed activity signal. Clamp result to [bearing_fatigue_life_min, bearing_fatigue_life_max].

grease_condition_degradation: Decrease by grease_degradation_rate * thermal_stress_and_oxidation_driver * dt, where thermal_stress_and_oxidation_driver is the normalized thermal stress and oxidation activity signal. Clamp result to [grease_condition_min, grease_condition_max].

cage_material_wear: Decrease by contamination_buildup_rate * metallic_wear_particles_driver * dt, where metallic_wear_particles_driver is the normalized metallic wear particle generation signal (coupled to cage wear severity). Clamp result to [cage_material_wear_min, cage_material_wear_max].

lubrication_film_tracked: Approach lubrication_film input using first-order lag: lubrication_film_tracked_new = lubrication_film_tracked + lubrication_film_lag_coefficient * (lubrication_film - lubrication_film_tracked). lubrication_film_lag_coefficient is capped at 0.5 for numerical stability. Clamp result to [0.0, 77.0].
output_logic: This component has no output ports as specified in the triage entry. All degradation information is exposed through states and events only.
event_logic: flaking_one_side_raceway: event_state = (bearing_fatigue_life - (1.0 - critical_bearing_wear)) / (1.0 - (1.0 - critical_bearing_wear)). Equals 1.0 when bearing_fatigue_life is healthy (1.0); reaches 0.0 when bearing_fatigue_life falls to (1.0 - critical_bearing_wear). Event triggers when event_state <= 0.0.

scoring_raceway_rolling_surface: event_state = min((grease_condition_degradation - (1.0 - critical_grease_degradation)) / (1.0 - (1.0 - critical_grease_degradation)), (lubrication_film_tracked - min_adequate_lubrication_film) / min_adequate_lubrication_film). Equals 1.0 under healthy conditions; reaches 0.0 when both grease is critically degraded and lubrication film is below minimum. Event triggers when event_state <= 0.0.

rib_scoring: event_state = min((grease_condition_degradation - (1.0 - critical_grease_degradation)) / (1.0 - (1.0 - critical_grease_degradation)), (cage_material_wear - (1.0 - critical_cage_wear)) / (1.0 - (1.0 - critical_cage_wear))). Equals 1.0 under healthy conditions; reaches 0.0 when both grease and cage are critically degraded. Event triggers when event_state <= 0.0.

excessive_preload: event_state = min((bearing_fatigue_life - (1.0 - critical_bearing_wear)) / (1.0 - (1.0 - critical_bearing_wear)), (cage_material_wear - (1.0 - critical_cage_wear)) / (1.0 - (1.0 - critical_cage_wear))). Equals 1.0 under healthy conditions; reaches 0.0 when both bearing fatigue life and cage wear are at critical levels. Event triggers when event_state <= 0.0.

cage_noise: event_state = min((cage_material_wear - (1.0 - critical_cage_wear)) / (1.0 - (1.0 - critical_cage_wear)), (grease_condition_degradation - (1.0 - cage_noise_grease_threshold)) / (1.0 - (1.0 - cage_noise_grease_threshold))). Equals 1.0 under healthy conditions; reaches 0.0 when cage wear and grease degradation both exceed their respective maintenance thresholds. Event triggers when event_state <= 0.0.
assumptions: bearing_fatigue_life is reformulated from the triage initial_value of 1.0 (new) degrading to 0.0 (end-of-life) to conform to the degradation state_type convention. The triage entry listed initial_value 1.0 which is consistent.

grease_condition_degradation and cage_material_wear are reformulated from the triage initial_value of 0.0 (interpreted as 'no degradation accumulated') to initial_value 1.0 (full health) degrading to 0.0 (fully degraded), conforming to the degradation state_type convention. The triage descriptions implied accumulation semantics but the degradation state_type is more physically appropriate for ProgPy.

The simulator ground truth provides bearing_wear_rate=0.02 matched to bearing_fatigue_life via load_cycles_and_speed driver, grease_degradation_rate=0.03 matched to grease_condition_degradation via thermal_stress_and_oxidation driver, and contamination_buildup_rate=0.01 matched to cage_material_wear via metallic_wear_particles driver (semantic match: cage wear generates contamination particles).

preload_degradation_rate=0.015 and preload_degradation state from the simulator ground truth drivers list do not have a direct named candidate state in the triage entry. The excessive_preload event is instead modeled through the combination of bearing_fatigue_life and cage_material_wear degradation, which physically cause clearance loss and preload increase. This is noted as a simplification; a dedicated preload_degradation state could be added if the simulator exposes it explicitly.
critical_bearing_wear=0.8 from the simulator is interpreted as: the event triggers when (1 - bearing_fatigue_life) >= 0.8, i.e., bearing_fatigue_life <= 0.2.

critical_grease_degradation threshold (0.7) and critical_cage_wear threshold (0.75) are derived from physical reasoning since no exact simulator ground truth values are provided for these states by name. They are exposed as tunable parameters.

lubrication_film_tracked is added as a tracking state to allow event logic to reference lubrication film levels without directly referencing the raw input port, satisfying the strict rule that threshold_states must contain only state names.
The component has no output ports as specified in the triage entry. Output logic section is therefore a no-op placeholder.

load_cycles_and_speed_driver, thermal_stress_and_oxidation_driver, and metallic_wear_particles_driver are assumed to be normalized scalar signals (0.0 to 1.0) provided by the simulator environment or computed from available inputs. Since lubrication_film is the only input port, these drivers must be supplied externally or approximated from lubrication_film in a full implementation.

overheat_threshold=140.0, speed_arrival_threshold=15.0, zero_speed_threshold=0.75, vibration thresholds, acceleration thresholds, critical_temperature=150.0, critical_vibration=10.7, and critical_position_error=2000.0 from the simulator ground truth are not directly applicable to this component's states or events and are not included to avoid scope creep.
